title: cerastium arvense subsp. maximum
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. maximum, also known as Field Chickweed or Greater Chickweed, is a perennial plant in the carnation family that grows in fields, meadows, and along roadsides. The medicinal properties of Field Chickweed include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and minor wounds. The health benefits of Field Chickweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. Field Chickweed can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or ointment, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a variety of skin and wound conditions.
